name: Samara Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: loyalty, honesty and the waters of the Volga

description: Three equal horizontal stripes of red, white and blue.
history: The flag of Samara Oblast was adopted on 13 October 1998. The colours were drawn from the historical banner of Samara Governorate granted in 1878.

features.symbolism: the red, white and blue tricolour reproduces the historical colours of the Samara provincial banner of 1878 and echoes the Pan-Slavic tricolour.
